preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar stop [k] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a multi-stage lenition process. This transformation typically involves voicing to [ɡ] and spirantization to [ɣ] before the sound fully vocalizes into a glide, often observed in intervocalic positions or following back vowels.

examples.note: Stages in the weakening of velars where voiceless stops eventually surfaced as glides in specific dialectal environments.
Weakening of labialized velars into pure glides in intervocalic positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
